skills.name: start-work
skills.description: Use when starting a new feature, unclear request, or multi-step task.

skills.name: shape-work
skills.description: Use when a product or technical request is vague, exploratory, or not ready for implementation planning.

skills.name: plan-work
skills.description: Use when requirements exist and a multi-step implementation needs sequencing.

skills.name: execute-work
skills.description: Use when a written implementation plan is ready; executes task-by-task with review and verification gates.

skills.name: auto-dev-loop
skills.description: Use when the user wants an autonomous end-to-end development loop that runs until verified complete or blocked. Do not use for simple questions, one-off reviews, or plan-only requests.

skills.name: worktree-work
skills.description: Use when creating, selecting, verifying, or cleaning up an isolated git worktree or branch for development work.

skills.name: orchestrate-agents
skills.description: Use when coordinating multiple agents or parallel workstreams across research, implementation, review, QA, or documentation.

skills.name: debug-root-cause
skills.description: Use for bugs, regressions, failing tests, build failures, production issues, or unexpected behavior. Finds root cause before any fix.

skills.name: verify-work
skills.description: Use before claiming work is done, fixed, passing, ready, reviewed, deployed, or complete.

skills.name: simplify-work
skills.description: Use to simplify implementation, reduce complexity, refactor for readability, clean technical debt, or make code easier to maintain.

skills.name: capture-learning
skills.description: Use to document code entry points, store durable project knowledge, capture root causes, preserve conventions, or map unfamiliar modules.

skills.name: codebase-map
skills.description: Use when onboarding to an unfamiliar repo, preparing a significant refactor, doing impact analysis, or explaining how a codebase/module works before changing it.

skills.name: project-knowledge
skills.description: Use to initialize, update, query, or maintain a repo-local project knowledge base that supports other dev-kit skills with verified architecture, decisions, conventions, and module maps.

skills.name: context-handoff
skills.description: Use when saving or restoring one-off working context for a paused task, long session, context-limit handoff, or another agent taking over.

skills.name: finish-work
skills.description: Use when a branch or task is ready for final review, commit, PR, or handoff.

skills.name: review-work
skills.description: Use for code review, diff review, implementation review, or checking work against a plan before finishing. Do not use for root-cause debugging or vague product shaping.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Use when creating, editing, reviewing, or improving skills.

skills.name: tdd-work
skills.description: Use when implementing new behavior, fixing bugs, or refactoring behavior.

skills.name: review-feedback
skills.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, PR comments, outside model suggestions, or user critique.

skills.name: docs-review
skills.description: Use when reviewing or improving README files, install docs, skill docs, guides, API docs, or agent-facing documentation.

skills.name: dependency-work
skills.description: Use when adding, removing, updating, auditing, or diagnosing project dependencies and lockfiles.

skills.name: seed-data-work
skills.description: Use when creating, updating, or verifying local development seed data, test fixtures, sample SQL inserts, or backend data setup for any database-backed project. Do not use for production data changes.

skills.name: release-notes
skills.description: Use when writing changelog entries, release notes, migration notes, or upgrade guidance from completed changes.

skills.name: onboard-project
skills.description: Run after install to tailor dev-kit to this project: detects stack, generates project-specific CLAUDE.md content, per-skill configs, and a routing map. Re-run after major refactors.
